locked_parameters.graph / SWIG: binary-treatment unconfoundedness SWIG with observed O=(Y,D,X), D in {0,1}, finite-support X, potential outcomes Y(1),Y(0), and no interference.
locked_parameters.treatment: the binary intervention do(D=d), d in {0,1}.
locked_parameters.identifying assumptions: consistency Y=DY(1)+(1-D)Y(0), unconfoundedness (Y(1),Y(0)) independent of D conditional on X, fixed-overlap epsilon <= p_0(X) <= 1-epsilon, finite fourth moments, and finite-support triangular-array local pseudo-truth sequences for the outcome regression and propensity/Riesz nuisance functions.
locked_parameters.estimator reports compared: the DML cross-fit AIPW report of ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ with a propensity-induced Riesz term; the CEINR locally-robust GMM ATE report whose scalar moment is psi_AIPW - A_LRO M_LRO^{-1} b_LRO, where A_LRO is the partial eta derivative with theta held fixed before the solved pseudo-target is plugged in, with Jacobian one and sandwich variance E[(psi_AIPW - A_LRO M_LRO^{-1} b_LRO)^2]; and the ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML automatic Riesz report whose Riesz pseudo-truth solves its own finite-support normal equations rather than being forced to equal the propensity-logit inverse-weight map.
locked_parameters.feature-span convention: B is fixed within the finite-support experiment and its treatment-specific span C(D,X) contains the baseline ATE Riesz representer alpha_0, so alpha_0(D,X)=C(D,X)' zeta_0 for a unique baseline coefficient zeta_0 solving the ADML normal equations at P_0.
locked_parameters.equivalence notion: uniform root-n asymptotic linearity around the same ATE and equality of the first-order studentized Wald law under the named variance report, meaning both numerator drift cancellation and report variance-ratio convergence to one, uniformly over the locked finite-support local experiment.

named_assumptions.label: ass:ate-id
named_assumptions.statement: Consistency \(Y=DY(1)+(1-D)Y(0)\), conditional unconfoundedness \((Y(1),Y(0))\perp D\mid X\), and fixed overlap \(0<\epsilon\le p_0(X)\le 1-\epsilon<1\) hold on the finite support \(\mathcal X_J\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:finite-regular
named_assumptions.statement: The support size \(J\) is fixed within each local experiment, every support cell has probability at least \(\epsilon_J>0\), \(\E[Y^4\mid D=d,X=x]\) is uniformly bounded, and \(\sigma_d^2(x)\) is finite for all \(d,x\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:report-maps
named_assumptions.statement: The DML, LRO, and ADML reports are the locked published reports as specialized in Section~5: DML uses the cross-fit AIPW score with propensity-induced \(\bar\alpha^p\) and pseudo-target \(\theta_{\mathrm{DML}}\); LRO uses the scalar CEINR moment \(\psi_{\mathrm{AIPW}}-A_{\mathrm{LRO}}M_{\mathrm{LRO}}^{-1}b_{\mathrm{LRO}}\), where \(A_{\mathrm{LRO}}\) is the partial \(\eta\)-derivative with \(\theta\) held fixed before \(\theta_{\mathrm{DML}}(\eta)\) is plugged in, Jacobian \(J_{\mathrm{LRO}}=1\), pseudo-target \(\theta_{\mathrm{LRO}}\), and sandwich variance \(V_{\mathrm{LRO}}\); ADML uses the automatic Riesz score and robust variance with pseudo-target \(\theta_{\mathrm{ADML}}\) and \(\bar\alpha^A=C^\transpose\bar\zeta\) solving \(\E[CC^\transpose]\bar\zeta=c_A\). The matrix \(M_{\mathrm{LRO}}(\eta_0)\) is nonsingular, the ADML Riesz Gram matrix \(\E[CC^\transpose]\) is nonsingular on the selected feature span, \(C^\transpose\zeta_0=\alpha_0\) at the baseline law, and the three report variances at the truth are positive.

named_assumptions.label: ass:pseudotruth-rates
named_assumptions.statement: There are deterministic finite-support directions \(h_g\in\mathcal H_J^2\), \(h^p_a,h^A_a\in\mathcal H_J\), exponents \(r_g,r^p_a,r^A_a\ge0\), and bounded remainders such that \(g_n=n^{-r_g}h_g+o(n^{-r_g})\), \(a^p_n=n^{-r^p_a}h^p_a+o(n^{-r^p_a})\), and \(a^A_n=n^{-r^A_a}h^A_a+o(n^{-r^A_a})\) in \(\|\cdot\|_\nu\). For report \(m\), write \((h_{a,m},r_{a,m})=(h^p_a,r^p_a)\) for DML and LRO and \((h_{a,m},r_{a,m})=(h^A_a,r^A_a)\) for ADML.

named_assumptions.label: ass:compatibility
named_assumptions.statement: The tuple \((h_g,h^p_a,h^A_a)\) belongs to the observable compatibility cone \(\mathcal C(P_0)\subseteq\mathcal H_J^2\times\mathcal H_J\times\mathcal H_J\) if and only if there exists a mean-zero local law score \(s(O)\) and coefficient differentials \((\delta\beta_1,\delta\beta_0,\delta\gamma,\delta\zeta)\) satisfying the displayed linearized regression, propensity-logit, and ADML Riesz normal equations, with the same \(s(O)\) solving all three systems.

named_assumptions.label: ass:expansion-domain
named_assumptions.statement: For every \(m\in\{\mathrm{DML},\mathrm{LRO},\mathrm{ADML}\}\), the numerator report is centered at its report-specific pseudo-target \(\theta_m\) from Section~5 and has an expansion with canonical leading term \(n^{-1/2}\sum_i\phi_0(O_i)\) plus the deterministic drift \(R_m(g_n,a_{m,n})\) and an \(o_{\Pr}(n^{-1/2})\) empirical-process remainder uniformly over the local experiment. Its named variance report, evaluated at the same solved report moment, satisfies the variance-ratio expansion \(V_m(g_n,a_{m,n})/V_0=1+S_m(g_n,a_{m,n})+o_{\Pr}(1)\) uniformly over the same experiment.

conjectures.label: partition
conjectures.statement:
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:ate-id}--\ref{ass:expansion-domain}, there exist explicit finite-dimensional objects
\[
\{\ell_{g,m},\ell_{a,m},T_m,s_{g,m},s_{a,m},U_m\}_{m\in\{\mathrm{DML},\mathrm{LRO},\mathrm{ADML}\}}
\quad\text{and}\quad \mathcal C(P_0)
\]
computable from the locked published report formulas and the observable finite-support law such that, for every rate tuple \((r_g,r^p_a,r^A_a)\) and compatible direction \((h_g,h^p_a,h^A_a)\), report \(m\) has the locked first-order studentized Wald law if and only if both
\[
n^{1/2-r_g}\ell_{g,m}(h_g)+n^{1/2-r_{a,m}}\ell_{a,m}(h_{a,m})
+n^{1/2-r_g-r_{a,m}}\langle T_mh_g,h_{a,m}\rangle_\nu \to 0
\]
and
\[
n^{-r_g}s_{g,m}(h_g)+n^{-r_{a,m}}s_{a,m}(h_{a,m})
+n^{-r_g-r_{a,m}}\langle U_mh_g,h_{a,m}\rangle_\nu \to 0 .
\]
The first display is the numerator-drift condition, and the second is the variance-report ratio condition for the named report.  The common-valid, estimator-specific-valid, first-order-separated, variance-report-separated, and jointly invalid regions are exactly the cells of these two conditions after intersecting with \(\mathcal C(P_0)\).  For an open set of finite-support laws, \(\mathcal C(P_0)\) is not a Cartesian product of outcome, propensity-induced Riesz, and automatic-Riesz direction sets, and the ADML-valid cell strictly contains a DML-valid cell along at least one one-good-nuisance face because \(h^A_a\) is constrained by the automatic Riesz normal equations while \(h^p_a\) is constrained by the propensity-logit pseudo-truth.  On another open set, DML and LRO have the same numerator-drift cell but different variance-report cells through \(S_{\mathrm{DML}}\ne S_{\mathrm{LRO}}\).

conjectures.label: witness
conjectures.statement: There is a nondegenerate finite-support law with \(J\le4\), positive overlap, and positive residual variance on all treatment cells such that the compatibility cone \(\mathcal C(P_0)\) contains two directions \((h_g,h^p_a,h^A_a)\) and \((\tilde h_g,\tilde h^p_a,\tilde h^A_a)\) with identical marginal norms and rate exponents but different products \(\langle T_mh_g,h_{a,m}\rangle_\nu\) or \(\langle U_mh_g,h_{a,m}\rangle_\nu\).  Along these two directions, at least one named report switches between numerator validity, variance-report validity, and first-order separation while the marginal rate tuple is unchanged.

supporting_theorems.label: product-guardrail
supporting_theorems.statement:
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:ate-id}, \ref{ass:finite-regular}, \ref{ass:report-maps}, \ref{ass:pseudotruth-rates}, and \ref{ass:expansion-domain}, if
\[
\sqrt n\,\|g_n\|_\nu\|a_{m,n}\|_\nu\to0,\qquad
\sqrt n\,|\ell_{g,m}(g_n)+\ell_{a,m}(a_{m,n})|\to0,\qquad
|S_m(g_n,a_{m,n})|\to0
\]
for each \(m\in\{\mathrm{DML},\mathrm{LRO},\mathrm{ADML}\}\), then the three named reports are uniformly root-\(n\) asymptotically linear with influence function \(\phi_0\) and have the same first-order Wald law with variance \(\E[\phi_0^2]\).
supporting_theorems.proof_tex:
\begin{theorem}[Theorem 1: product-rate common-EIF guardrail]\label{thm:product-guardrail}
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:ate-id}, \ref{ass:finite-regular}, \ref{ass:report-maps}, \ref{ass:pseudotruth-rates}, and \ref{ass:expansion-domain}, if
\[
\sqrt n\,\|g_n\|_\nu\|a_{m,n}\|_\nu\to0,\qquad
\sqrt n\,|\ell_{g,m}(g_n)+\ell_{a,m}(a_{m,n})|\to0,\qquad
|S_m(g_n,a_{m,n})|\to0
\]
for each \(m\in\{\mathrm{DML},\mathrm{LRO},\mathrm{ADML}\}\), then the three named reports are uniformly root-\(n\) asymptotically linear with influence function \(\phi_0\) and have the same first-order Wald law with variance \(\E[\phi_0^2]\).
\end{theorem}
\begin{proof}
Fix \(m\in\{\mathrm{DML},\mathrm{LRO},\mathrm{ADML}\}\).  By Assumption~\ref{ass:expansion-domain}, the centered numerator report admits the uniform expansion
\[
\hat\theta_m-\theta_0=n^{-1}\sum_{i=1}^n\phi_0(O_i)+R_m(g_n,a_{m,n})+o_{\Pr}(n^{-1/2}).
\]
The drift decomposition from Section~7 gives
\[
R_m(g_n,a_{m,n})=\ell_{g,m}(g_n)+\ell_{a,m}(a_{m,n})+\langle T_mg_n,a_{m,n}\rangle_\nu.
\]
Because \(T_m\) is a fixed finite-dimensional linear map under Assumptions~\ref{ass:finite-regular} and \ref{ass:report-maps}, its operator norm is finite; call it \(K_m\).  Cauchy's inequality for \(\langle\cdot,\cdot\rangle_\nu\) yields
\[
|\langle T_mg_n,a_{m,n}\rangle_\nu|\le K_m\|g_n\|_\nu\|a_{m,n}\|_\nu.
\]
The two numerator hypotheses therefore imply
\[
\sqrt n\,R_m(g_n,a_{m,n})
=\sqrt n\{\ell_{g,m}(g_n)+\ell_{a,m}(a_{m,n})\}+O(\sqrt n\|g_n\|_\nu\|a_{m,n}\|_\nu)\to0.
\]
Multiplying the expansion by \(\sqrt n\) gives
\[
\sqrt n(\hat\theta_m-\theta_0)=n^{-1/2}\sum_{i=1}^n\phi_0(O_i)+o_{\Pr}(1)
\]
uniformly over the locked local experiment.  Assumptions~\ref{ass:ate-id} and \ref{ass:finite-regular} give \(\E[\phi_0]=0\), finite second moment, and positive variance \(V_0=\E[\phi_0^2]\); hence the common influence function is \(\phi_0\).  The variance-report expansion in Assumption~\ref{ass:expansion-domain} gives
\[
V_m(g_n,a_{m,n})/V_0=1+S_m(g_n,a_{m,n})+o_{\Pr}(1).
\]
The hypothesis \(|S_m(g_n,a_{m,n})|\to0\) implies \(V_m(g_n,a_{m,n})/V_0\to1\).  Since Assumption~\ref{ass:report-maps} states that the truth-level report variances are positive, Slutsky's theorem applied to the preceding asymptotic linear expansion gives the same first-order studentized Wald law for report \(m\), with variance \(V_0=\E[\phi_0^2]\).  The argument did not use any report-specific property beyond the locked report maps and expansion-domain hypotheses, so it holds for DML, LRO, and ADML simultaneously.\end{proof}
